001|use std::fs;
002|use std::io::Write;
003|use std::os::fd::{AsRawFd, OwnedFd};
004|use std::path::PathBuf;
005|use std::rc::Rc;
006|
007|use libcgroups::common::CgroupManager;
008|use nix::unistd::Pid;
009|use oci_spec::runtime::Spec;
010|
011|use super::{Container, ContainerStatus};
012|use crate::error::{CreateContainerError, LibcontainerError, MissingSpecError};
013|use crate::notify_socket::NotifyListener;
014|use crate::process::args::{ContainerArgs, ContainerType};
015|use crate::process::intel_rdt::delete_resctrl_subdirectory;
016|use crate::process::{self};
017|use crate::syscall::syscall::SyscallType;
018|use crate::user_ns::UserNamespaceConfig;
019|use crate::workload::Executor;
020|use crate::{hooks, utils};
021|
022|pub(super) struct ContainerBuilderImpl {
023|    /// Flag indicating if an init or a tenant container should be created
024|    pub container_type: ContainerType,
025|    /// Interface to operating system primitives
026|    pub syscall: SyscallType,
027|    /// Flag indicating if systemd should be used for cgroup management
028|    pub use_systemd: bool,
029|    /// Id of the container
030|    pub container_id: String,
031|    /// OCI compliant runtime spec
032|    pub spec: Rc<Spec>,
033|    /// Root filesystem of the container
034|    pub rootfs: PathBuf,
035|    /// File which will be used to communicate the pid of the
036|    /// container process to the higher level runtime
037|    pub pid_file: Option<PathBuf>,
038|    /// Socket to communicate the file descriptor of the ptty
039|    pub console_socket: Option<OwnedFd>,
040|    /// Options for new user namespace
041|    pub user_ns_config: Option<UserNamespaceConfig>,
042|    /// Path to the Unix Domain Socket to communicate container start
043|    pub notify_path: PathBuf,
044|    /// Container state
045|    pub container: Option<Container>,
046|    /// File descriptos preserved/passed to the container init process.
047|    pub preserve_fds: i32,
048|    /// If the container is to be run in detached mode
049|    pub detached: bool,
050|    /// Default executes the specified execution of a generic command
051|    pub executor: Box<dyn Executor>,
052|    /// If do not use pivot root to jail process inside rootfs
053|    pub no_pivot: bool,
054|    // RawFd set to stdin of the container init process.
055|    pub stdin: Option<OwnedFd>,
056|    // RawFd set to stdout of the container init process.
057|    pub stdout: Option<OwnedFd>,
058|    // RawFd set to stderr of the container init process.
059|    pub stderr: Option<OwnedFd>,
060|    // Indicate if the init process should be a sibling of the main process.
061|    pub as_sibling: bool,
062|}
063|
064|impl ContainerBuilderImpl {
065|    pub(super) fn create(&mut self) -> Result<Pid, LibcontainerError> {
066|        match self.run_container() {
067|            Ok(pid) => Ok(pid),
068|            Err(outer) => {
069|                // Only the init container should be cleaned up in the case of
070|                // an error.
071|                let cleanup_err = if self.is_init_container() {
072|                    self.cleanup_container().err()
073|                } else {
074|                    None
075|                };
076|
077|                Err(CreateContainerError::new(outer, cleanup_err).into())
078|            }
079|        }
080|    }
081|
082|    fn is_init_container(&self) -> bool {
083|        matches!(self.container_type, ContainerType::InitContainer)
084|    }
085|
086|    fn run_container(&mut self) -> Result<Pid, LibcontainerError> {
087|        let linux = self.spec.linux().as_ref().ok_or(MissingSpecError::Linux)?;
088|        let cgroups_path = utils::get_cgroup_path(linux.cgroups_path(), &self.container_id);
089|        let cgroup_config = libcgroups::common::CgroupConfig {
090|            cgroup_path: cgroups_path,
091|            systemd_cgroup: self.use_systemd || self.user_ns_config.is_some(),
092|            container_name: self.container_id.to_owned(),
093|        };
094|        let process = self
095|            .spec
096|            .process()
097|            .as_ref()
098|            .ok_or(MissingSpecError::Process)?;
099|
100|        // Need to create the notify socket before we pivot root, since the unix
101|        // domain socket used here is outside of the rootfs of container. During
102|        // exec, need to create the socket before we enter into existing mount
103|        // namespace. We also need to create to socket before entering into the
104|        // user namespace in the case that the path is located in paths only
105|        // root can access.
106|        let notify_listener = NotifyListener::new(&self.notify_path)?;
107|
108|        // If Out-of-memory score adjustment is set in specification.  set the score
109|        // value for the current process check
110|        // https://dev.to/rrampage/surviving-the-linux-oom-killer-2ki9 for some more
111|        // information.
112|        //
113|        // This has to be done before !dumpable because /proc/self/oom_score_adj
114|        // is not writeable unless you're an privileged user (if !dumpable is
115|        // set). All children inherit their parent's oom_score_adj value on
116|        // fork(2) so this will always be propagated properly.
117|        if let Some(oom_score_adj) = process.oom_score_adj() {
118|            tracing::debug!("Set OOM score to {}", oom_score_adj);
119|            let mut f = fs::File::create("/proc/self/oom_score_adj").map_err(|err| {
120|                tracing::error!("failed to open /proc/self/oom_score_adj: {}", err);
121|                LibcontainerError::OtherIO(err)
122|            })?;
123|            f.write_all(oom_score_adj.to_string().as_bytes())
124|                .map_err(|err| {
125|                    tracing::error!("failed to write to /proc/self/oom_score_adj: {}", err);
126|                    LibcontainerError::OtherIO(err)
127|                })?;
128|        }
129|
130|        // Make the process non-dumpable, to avoid various race conditions that
131|        // could cause processes in namespaces we're joining to access host
132|        // resources (or potentially execute code).
133|        //
134|        // However, if the number of namespaces we are joining is 0, we are not
135|        // going to be switching to a different security context. Thus setting
136|        // ourselves to be non-dumpable only breaks things (like rootless
137|        // containers), which is the recommendation from the kernel folks.
138|        if linux.namespaces().is_some() {
139|            prctl::set_dumpable(false).map_err(|e| {
140|                LibcontainerError::Other(format!(
141|                    "error in setting dumpable to false : {}",
142|                    nix::errno::Errno::from_raw(e)
143|                ))
144|            })?;
145|        }
146|
147|        // This container_args will be passed to the container processes,
148|        // therefore we will have to move all the variable by value. Since self
149|        // is a shared reference, we have to clone these variables here.
150|        let container_args = ContainerArgs {
151|            container_type: self.container_type,
152|            syscall: self.syscall,
153|            spec: Rc::clone(&self.spec),
154|            rootfs: self.rootfs.to_owned(),
155|            console_socket: self.console_socket.as_ref().map(|c| c.as_raw_fd()),
156|            notify_listener,
157|            preserve_fds: self.preserve_fds,
158|            container: self.container.to_owned(),
159|            user_ns_config: self.user_ns_config.to_owned(),
160|            cgroup_config,
161|            detached: self.detached,
162|            executor: self.executor.clone(),
163|            no_pivot: self.no_pivot,
164|            stdin: self.stdin.as_ref().map(|x| x.as_raw_fd()),
165|            stdout: self.stdout.as_ref().map(|x| x.as_raw_fd()),
166|            stderr: self.stderr.as_ref().map(|x| x.as_raw_fd()),
167|            as_sibling: self.as_sibling,
168|        };
169|
170|        let (init_pid, need_to_clean_up_intel_rdt_dir) =
171|            process::container_main_process::container_main_process(&container_args).map_err(
172|                |err| {
173|                    tracing::error!("failed to run container process {}", err);
174|                    LibcontainerError::MainProcess(err)
175|                },
176|            )?;
177|
178|        // if file to write the pid to is specified, write pid of the child
179|        if let Some(pid_file) = &self.pid_file {
180|            fs::write(pid_file, format!("{init_pid}")).map_err(|err| {
181|                tracing::error!("failed to write pid to file: {}", err);
182|                LibcontainerError::OtherIO(err)
183|            })?;
184|        }
185|
186|        if let Some(container) = &mut self.container {
187|            // update status and pid of the container process
188|            container
189|                .set_status(ContainerStatus::Created)
190|                .set_creator(nix::unistd::geteuid().as_raw())
191|                .set_pid(init_pid.as_raw())
192|                .set_clean_up_intel_rdt_directory(need_to_clean_up_intel_rdt_dir)
193|                .save()?;
194|        }
195|
196|        if matches!(self.container_type, ContainerType::InitContainer) {
197|            if let Some(hooks) = self.spec.hooks() {
198|                hooks::run_hooks(
199|                    hooks.create_runtime().as_ref(),
200|                    self.container.as_ref(),
201|                    None,
202|                )?
203|            }
204|        }
205|
206|        Ok(init_pid)
207|    }
208|
209|    fn cleanup_container(&self) -> Result<(), LibcontainerError> {
210|        let linux = self.spec.linux().as_ref().ok_or(MissingSpecError::Linux)?;
211|        let cgroups_path = utils::get_cgroup_path(linux.cgroups_path(), &self.container_id);
212|        let cmanager =
213|            libcgroups::common::create_cgroup_manager(libcgroups::common::CgroupConfig {
214|                cgroup_path: cgroups_path,
215|                systemd_cgroup: self.use_systemd || self.user_ns_config.is_some(),
216|                container_name: self.container_id.to_string(),
217|            })?;
218|
219|        let mut errors = Vec::new();
220|
221|        if let Err(e) = cmanager.remove() {
222|            tracing::error!(error = ?e, "failed to remove cgroup manager");
223|            errors.push(e.to_string());
224|        }
225|
226|        if let Some(container) = &self.container {
227|            if let Some(true) = container.clean_up_intel_rdt_subdirectory() {
228|                if let Err(e) = delete_resctrl_subdirectory(container.id()) {
229|                    tracing::error!(id = ?container.id(), error = ?e, "failed to delete resctrl subdirectory");
230|                    errors.push(e.to_string());
231|                }
232|            }
233|
234|            if container.root.exists() {
235|                if let Err(e) = fs::remove_dir_all(&container.root) {
236|                    tracing::error!(container_root = ?container.root, error = ?e, "failed to delete container root");
237|                    errors.push(e.to_string());
238|                }
239|            }
240|        }
241|
242|        if !errors.is_empty() {
243|            return Err(LibcontainerError::Other(format!(
244|                "failed to cleanup container: {}",
245|                errors.join(";")
246|            )));
247|        }
248|
249|        Ok(())
250|    }
251|}

use std::fs;
use std::io::Write;
use std::os::fd::{AsRawFd, OwnedFd};
use std::path::PathBuf;
use std::rc::Rc;

use libcgroups::common::CgroupManager;
use nix::unistd::Pid;
use oci_spec::runtime::Spec;

use super::{Container, ContainerStatus};
use crate::error::{CreateContainerError, LibcontainerError, MissingSpecError};
use crate::notify_socket::NotifyListener;
use crate::process::args::{ContainerArgs, ContainerType};
use crate::process::intel_rdt::delete_resctrl_subdirectory;
use crate::process::{self};
use crate::syscall::syscall::SyscallType;
use crate::user_ns::UserNamespaceConfig;
use crate::workload::Executor;
use crate::{hooks, utils};

pub(super) struct ContainerBuilderImpl {
    /// Flag indicating if an init or a tenant container should be created
    pub container_type: ContainerType,
    /// Interface to operating system primitives
    pub syscall: SyscallType,
    /// Flag indicating if systemd should be used for cgroup management
    pub use_systemd: bool,
    /// Id of the container
    pub container_id: String,
    /// OCI compliant runtime spec
    pub spec: Rc<Spec>,
    /// Root filesystem of the container
    pub rootfs: PathBuf,
    /// File which will be used to communicate the pid of the
    /// container process to the higher level runtime
    pub pid_file: Option<PathBuf>,
    /// Socket to communicate the file descriptor of the ptty
    pub console_socket: Option<OwnedFd>,
    /// Options for new user namespace
    pub user_ns_config: Option<UserNamespaceConfig>,
    /// Path to the Unix Domain Socket to communicate container start
    pub notify_path: PathBuf,
    /// Container state
    pub container: Option<Container>,
    /// File descriptos preserved/passed to the container init process.
    pub preserve_fds: i32,
    /// If the container is to be run in detached mode
    pub detached: bool,
    /// Default executes the specified execution of a generic command
    pub executor: Box<dyn Executor>,
    /// If do not use pivot root to jail process inside rootfs
    pub no_pivot: bool,
    // RawFd set to stdin of the container init process.
    pub stdin: Option<OwnedFd>,
    // RawFd set to stdout of the container init process.
    pub stdout: Option<OwnedFd>,
    // RawFd set to stderr of the container init process.
    pub stderr: Option<OwnedFd>,
    // Indicate if the init process should be a sibling of the main process.
    pub as_sibling: bool,
}

impl ContainerBuilderImpl {
    pub(super) fn create(&mut self) -> Result<Pid, LibcontainerError> {
        match self.run_container() {
            Ok(pid) => Ok(pid),
            Err(outer) => {
                // Only the init container should be cleaned up in the case of
                // an error.
                let cleanup_err = if self.is_init_container() {
                    self.cleanup_container().err()
                } else {
                    None
                };

                Err(CreateContainerError::new(outer, cleanup_err).into())
            }
        }
    }

    fn is_init_container(&self) -> bool {
        matches!(self.container_type, ContainerType::InitContainer)
    }

    fn run_container(&mut self) -> Result<Pid, LibcontainerError> {
        let linux = self.spec.linux().as_ref().ok_or(MissingSpecError::Linux)?;
        let cgroups_path = utils::get_cgroup_path(linux.cgroups_path(), &self.container_id);
        let cgroup_config = libcgroups::common::CgroupConfig {
            cgroup_path: cgroups_path,
            systemd_cgroup: self.use_systemd || self.user_ns_config.is_some(),
            container_name: self.container_id.to_owned(),
        };
        let process = self
            .spec
            .process()
            .as_ref()
            .ok_or(MissingSpecError::Process)?;

        // Need to create the notify socket before we pivot root, since the unix
        // domain socket used here is outside of the rootfs of container. During
        // exec, need to create the socket before we enter into existing mount
        // namespace. We also need to create to socket before entering into the
        // user namespace in the case that the path is located in paths only
        // root can access.
        let notify_listener = NotifyListener::new(&self.notify_path)?;

        // If Out-of-memory score adjustment is set in specification.  set the score
        // value for the current process check
        // https://dev.to/rrampage/surviving-the-linux-oom-killer-2ki9 for some more
        // information.
        //
        // This has to be done before !dumpable because /proc/self/oom_score_adj
        // is not writeable unless you're an privileged user (if !dumpable is
        // set). All children inherit their parent's oom_score_adj value on
        // fork(2) so this will always be propagated properly.
        if let Some(oom_score_adj) = process.oom_score_adj() {
            tracing::debug!("Set OOM score to {}", oom_score_adj);
            let mut f = fs::File::create("/proc/self/oom_score_adj").map_err(|err| {
                tracing::error!("failed to open /proc/self/oom_score_adj: {}", err);
                LibcontainerError::OtherIO(err)
            })?;
            f.write_all(oom_score_adj.to_string().as_bytes())
                .map_err(|err| {
                    tracing::error!("failed to write to /proc/self/oom_score_adj: {}", err);
                    LibcontainerError::OtherIO(err)
                })?;
        }

        // Make the process non-dumpable, to avoid various race conditions that
        // could cause processes in namespaces we're joining to access host
        // resources (or potentially execute code).
        //
        // However, if the number of namespaces we are joining is 0, we are not
        // going to be switching to a different security context. Thus setting
        // ourselves to be non-dumpable only breaks things (like rootless
        // containers), which is the recommendation from the kernel folks.
        if linux.namespaces().is_some() {
            prctl::set_dumpable(false).map_err(|e| {
                LibcontainerError::Other(format!(
                    "error in setting dumpable to false : {}",
                    nix::errno::Errno::from_raw(e)
                ))
            })?;
        }

        // This container_args will be passed to the container processes,
        // therefore we will have to move all the variable by value. Since self
        // is a shared reference, we have to clone these variables here.
        let container_args = ContainerArgs {
            container_type: self.container_type,
            syscall: self.syscall,
            spec: Rc::clone(&self.spec),
            rootfs: self.rootfs.to_owned(),
            console_socket: self.console_socket.as_ref().map(|c| c.as_raw_fd()),
            notify_listener,
            preserve_fds: self.preserve_fds,
            container: self.container.to_owned(),
            user_ns_config: self.user_ns_config.to_owned(),
            cgroup_config,
            detached: self.detached,
            executor: self.executor.clone(),
            no_pivot: self.no_pivot,
            stdin: self.stdin.as_ref().map(|x| x.as_raw_fd()),
            stdout: self.stdout.as_ref().map(|x| x.as_raw_fd()),
            stderr: self.stderr.as_ref().map(|x| x.as_raw_fd()),
            as_sibling: self.as_sibling,
        };

        let (init_pid, need_to_clean_up_intel_rdt_dir) =
            process::container_main_process::container_main_process(&container_args).map_err(
                |err| {
                    tracing::error!("failed to run container process {}", err);
                    LibcontainerError::MainProcess(err)
                },
            )?;

        // if file to write the pid to is specified, write pid of the child
        if let Some(pid_file) = &self.pid_file {
            fs::write(pid_file, format!("{init_pid}")).map_err(|err| {
                tracing::error!("failed to write pid to file: {}", err);
                LibcontainerError::OtherIO(err)
            })?;
        }

        if let Some(container) = &mut self.container {
            // update status and pid of the container process
            container
                .set_status(ContainerStatus::Created)
                .set_creator(nix::unistd::geteuid().as_raw())
                .set_pid(init_pid.as_raw())
                .set_clean_up_intel_rdt_directory(need_to_clean_up_intel_rdt_dir)
                .save()?;
        }

        if matches!(self.container_type, ContainerType::InitContainer) {
            if let Some(hooks) = self.spec.hooks() {
                // Per OCI specification, prestart hooks must run before createRuntime hooks.
                // While prestart is marked deprecated, existing integrations still use it.
                #[allow(deprecated)]
                hooks::run_hooks(
                    hooks.prestart().as_ref(),
                    self.container.as_ref(),
                    None,
                )?;
                hooks::run_hooks(
                    hooks.create_runtime().as_ref(),
                    self.container.as_ref(),
                    None,
                )?
            }
        }

        Ok(init_pid)
    }

    fn cleanup_container(&self) -> Result<(), LibcontainerError> {
        let linux = self.spec.linux().as_ref().ok_or(MissingSpecError::Linux)?;
        let cgroups_path = utils::get_cgroup_path(linux.cgroups_path(), &self.container_id);
        let cmanager =
            libcgroups::common::create_cgroup_manager(libcgroups::common::CgroupConfig {
                cgroup_path: cgroups_path,
                systemd_cgroup: self.use_systemd || self.user_ns_config.is_some(),
                container_name: self.container_id.to_string(),
            })?;

        let mut errors = Vec::new();

        if let Err(e) = cmanager.remove() {
            tracing::error!(error = ?e, "failed to remove cgroup manager");
            errors.push(e.to_string());
        }

        if let Some(container) = &self.container {
            if let Some(true) = container.clean_up_intel_rdt_subdirectory() {
                if let Err(e) = delete_resctrl_subdirectory(container.id()) {
                    tracing::error!(id = ?container.id(), error = ?e, "failed to delete resctrl subdirectory");
                    errors.push(e.to_string());
                }
            }

            if container.root.exists() {
                if let Err(e) = fs::remove_dir_all(&container.root) {
                    tracing::error!(container_root = ?container.root, error = ?e, "failed to delete container root");
                    errors.push(e.to_string());
                }
            }
        }

        if !errors.is_empty() {
            return Err(LibcontainerError::Other(format!(
                "failed to cleanup container: {}",
                errors.join(";")
            )));
        }

        Ok(())
    }
}

use nix::sys::signal;

use super::{Container, ContainerStatus};
use crate::config::YoukiConfig;
use crate::error::LibcontainerError;
use crate::hooks;
use crate::notify_socket::{NOTIFY_FILE, NotifySocket};

impl Container {
    /// Starts a previously created container
    ///
    /// # Example
    ///
    /// ```no_run
    /// use libcontainer::container::builder::ContainerBuilder;
    /// use libcontainer::syscall::syscall::SyscallType;
    ///
    /// # fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {
    /// let mut container = ContainerBuilder::new(
    ///     "74f1a4cb3801".to_owned(),
    ///     SyscallType::default(),
    /// )
    /// .as_init("/var/run/docker/bundle")
    /// .build()?;
    ///
    /// container.start();
    /// # Ok(())
    /// # }
    /// ```
    pub fn start(&mut self) -> Result<(), LibcontainerError> {
        self.refresh_status()?;

        if !self.can_start() {
            tracing::error!(status = ?self.status(), id = ?self.id(), "cannot start container due to incorrect state");
            return Err(LibcontainerError::IncorrectStatus);
        }

        let config = YoukiConfig::load(&self.root).map_err(|err| {
            tracing::error!(
                "failed to load runtime spec for container {}: {}",
                self.id(),
                err
            );
            err
        })?;

        let mut notify_socket = NotifySocket::new(self.root.join(NOTIFY_FILE));
        notify_socket.notify_container_start()?;
        self.set_status(ContainerStatus::Running)
            .save()
            .map_err(|err| {
                tracing::error!(id = ?self.id(), ?err, "failed to save state for container");
                err
            })?;

        // Run post start hooks. It runs after the container process is started.
        // It is called in the runtime namespace.
        if let Some(hooks) = config.hooks.as_ref() {
            hooks::run_hooks(hooks.poststart().as_ref(), Some(self), Some(&self.root)).map_err(
                |err| {
                    tracing::error!("failed to run post start hooks: {}", err);
                    err
                },
            )?;
        }

        Ok(())
    }
}

014|    /// ```no_run
015|    /// use libcontainer::container::builder::ContainerBuilder;
016|    /// use libcontainer::syscall::syscall::SyscallType;
017|    ///
018|    /// # fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {
019|    /// let mut container = ContainerBuilder::new(
020|    ///     "74f1a4cb3801".to_owned(),
021|    ///     SyscallType::default(),
022|    /// )
023|    /// .as_init("/var/run/docker/bundle")
024|    /// .build()?;
025|    ///
026|    /// container.start();
027|    /// # Ok(())
028|    /// # }
029|    /// ```
030|    pub fn start(&mut self) -> Result<(), LibcontainerError> {
031|        self.refresh_status()?;
032|
033|        if !self.can_start() {
034|            tracing::error!(status = ?self.status(), id = ?self.id(), "cannot start container due to incorrect state");
035|            return Err(LibcontainerError::IncorrectStatus);
036|        }
037|
038|        let config = YoukiConfig::load(&self.root).map_err(|err| {
039|            tracing::error!(
040|                "failed to load runtime spec for container {}: {}",
041|                self.id(),
042|                err
043|            );
044|            err
045|        })?;
046|
047|        let mut notify_socket = NotifySocket::new(self.root.join(NOTIFY_FILE));
048|        notify_socket.notify_container_start()?;
049|        self.set_status(ContainerStatus::Running)
050|            .save()
051|            .map_err(|err| {
052|                tracing::error!(id = ?self.id(), ?err, "failed to save state for container");
053|                err
054|            })?;
055|
056|        // Run post start hooks. It runs after the container process is started.

058|        if let Some(hooks) = config.hooks.as_ref() {

060|                |err| {
061|                    tracing::error!("failed to run post start hooks: {}", err);
062|                    err
063|                },
064|            )?;
065|        }
066|
067|        Ok(())
068|    }

use super::{Container, ContainerStatus};
use crate::config::YoukiConfig;
use crate::error::LibcontainerError;
use crate::hooks;
use crate::notify_socket::{NOTIFY_FILE, NotifySocket};

impl Container {
    /// Starts a previously created container
    ///
    /// # Example
    ///
    /// ```no_run
    /// use libcontainer::container::builder::ContainerBuilder;
    /// use libcontainer::syscall::syscall::SyscallType;
    ///
    /// # fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {
    /// let mut container = ContainerBuilder::new(
    ///     "74f1a4cb3801".to_owned(),
    ///     SyscallType::default(),
    /// )
    /// .as_init("/var/run/docker/bundle")
    /// .build()?;
    ///
    /// container.start();
    /// # Ok(())
    /// # }
    /// ```
    pub fn start(&mut self) -> Result<(), LibcontainerError> {
        self.refresh_status()?;

        if !self.can_start() {
            tracing::error!(status = ?self.status(), id = ?self.id(), "cannot start container due to incorrect state");
            return Err(LibcontainerError::IncorrectStatus);
        }

        let config = YoukiConfig::load(&self.root).map_err(|err| {
            tracing::error!(
                "failed to load runtime spec for container {}: {}",
                self.id(),
                err
            );
            err
        })?;

        let mut notify_socket = NotifySocket::new(self.root.join(NOTIFY_FILE));
        notify_socket.notify_container_start()?;
        self.set_status(ContainerStatus::Running)
            .save()
            .map_err(|err| {
                tracing::error!(id = ?self.id(), ?err, "failed to save state for container");
                err
            })?;

        // Run post start hooks. It runs after the container process is started.
        // It is called in the runtime namespace.
        if let Some(hooks) = config.hooks.as_ref() {
            hooks::run_hooks(hooks.poststart().as_ref(), Some(self), Some(&self.root)).map_err(
                |err| {
                    tracing::error!("failed to run post start hooks: {}", err);
                    err
                },
            )?;
        }

        Ok(())
    }
}
